sections.content:
The game reset! The 3x3 grid is back to the initial state:
```
Row 49: GGgggGGWWWGGggg  (top row: gray, white, gray)
Row 54: GGWWWGGgggGGWWW  (middle row: white, gray, white)
Row 59: GGgggGGWWWGGggg  (bottom row: gray, white, gray)
```
And the bar is also back to the initial state:
```
Row 19: BBbSSSSggggggggggggggggggggggbbSSBB
Row 20: BBbSSSSggggggggggggggggggggggbbSSBB
Row 21: BBbSSSSBBBBBBggggggggggggggggbbSSBB
Row 22: BBbbbbbBBBBBBggggggggggggggggbbSSBB
```
So the game automatically reset after the game over state. The state is now NOT_FINISHED.
Now I understand the game better:
- The 3x3 grid has 4 white cells in an X pattern and 5 gray cells
- The goal is to click the 4 white cells to turn them green (matching the target pattern)
- Clicking gray cells causes a game over
- The target pattern is shown in the left panel (purple dots in X pattern)
Let me now click the 4 white cells to complete the level. The white cells are at:
- Top-center: row 50, col 30
- Middle-left: row 55, col 26
- Middle-right: row 55, col 34
- Bottom-center: row 60, col 30

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click the 4 white cells in X pattern to complete the level
white_cells = [
    (50, 30),  # top-center
    (55, 26),  # middle-left
    (55, 34),  # middle-right
    (60, 30),  # bottom-center
]
actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c} for r, c in white_cells]
action(actions)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
